highlights: Expanded the homepage hub with live links and clearer version labels for Feed, Bullion, Battalion, and Perihelion.
Tightened the service launcher and VS Code task stack so the active APIs and tunnels start with cleaner names and fewer dead terminals.
Continued turning the network into a documented platform with stronger runbooks, architecture notes, and cross-machine workflow guidance.
Promoted the shared auth system under the visible Multimillion name while keeping the stable JeffersonWM host and session plumbing underneath.
Kept the regular user experience simpler while preserving the fuller admin flow for approvals, notes, memberships, and account history.
Strengthened the cross-app identity layer that Perihelion and future apps can plug into without rebuilding account logic from scratch.
Established Clionidae as the new reusable JeffersonWM template with its own port range, standards contract, and integrated frontend-backend shell.
Added a live visual specimen with core UI primitives and basic card families so future apps start from a concrete design system instead of a blank page.
Fixed subfolder deployment for /clionidae/ and aligned the starter around Multimillion-aware auth, feed-ready conventions, and documented data options.
